entry.title: Re: S4HANA Public Cloud - how to integrate external datas...
entry.content:
@yschlaeppi ,

Thanks for the question. 

First and foremost, if you want to enrich the data in the form, the Data source of the standard form template should be available for extension in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can check this via app : Custom fields.

Also for example, check if the Business scenarios' like sales order/invoice is supported to have any to capture custom fields, 

Otherwise, If the data resides outside the Public Cloud system and you wanted to have a standalone form with the data coming from Public cloud and the delta data from other systems, then you may need to use SAP BTP form services to have a standalone form with enriched data. 

Please be noted that, you need to use the standard released artefacts like API/CDS/Business Objects etc (in public cloud) in order to populate the data and other services in SAP BTP to store the data coming from other systems. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/forms-service-by-adobe/sap-forms-service-cf/sap-forms-service-by-adobe 

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog/forms-service-by-adobe?region=all 

https://help.sap.com/docs/forms-service-by-adobe/sap-forms-service-neo/architecture 

Please have a look and let me know if you need any more information or provide with additional info to support you. Thanks. 

Best Regards,

Sri

entry.title: IGS very broken!
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In one of our systems, IGS is very broken and we need to fix it, however we're out of ideas...
Transaction SIGS says it can't connect to IGS. The RFC destination doesn't work:

 

How can we register it ? The reginfo and secinfo files allow every connection. We have tried restarting the Gateway in transaction SMGW without any success.

When running the command:

rfcexec -a IGS.<SID> -g hostname -x sapgw01

the RFC starts working, but as soon as we hit Ctrl+C to exit the process, the RFC stops working again.
And even while the RFC is working under the influence of the rfcexec command, the SIGS transaction is
showing the following error:

We keep searching on the SAP Marketplace and on google, but we can't find a solution that could help us to 
fix IGS in this particular system. This is an S/4 Hana 2023 system running on Linux. Please advise, many thanks!

entry.title: Re: How can I prevent materials with the deletion flag fr...
entry.content:
Hi Wendy, I ran your follow up question through our AI Consultant and got the below response.  I welcome the feedback, fee free to try our AI consultant on the Eerly Ai website

 

SAP AI Consultant

Based on the information provided, it appears that there is an inconsistency in the behavior of the "Create Sales Order" app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud when it comes to handling products marked for deletion. Here are some steps and considerations to address this issue:

1. Verify System Behavior

First, you need to verify if the behavior observed is consistent across different users and sessions. This will help determine if the issue is isolated or widespread.

2. Check SAP Notes and Knowledge Base Articles

You have already referred to SAP Knowledge Base Article 3111463, which indicates that products marked for deletion should not be available for selection via the search help/value help. Ensure you have the latest updates and patches applied to your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

3. SAP Support

If the behavior persists despite the system being up-to-date, it might be a bug. In such cases, it is advisable to open a support ticket with SAP. Provide them with detailed information, including:

  • The steps to reproduce the issue.
  • Screenshots (like the ones you mentioned) showing the discrepancy.
  • Reference to SAP Note 3111463.

4. Workarounds

While waiting for a resolution from SAP, you might consider the following workarounds:

  • Manual Filtering: Instruct users to manually filter out products marked for deletion when using the search help/value help.
  • Custom Development: If feasible, consider developing a custom enhancement or user exit to exclude products marked for deletion from the search help.

5. Configuration Check

Ensure that the configuration settings related to product deletion and search help are correctly set up. Sometimes, configuration inconsistencies can lead to unexpected behavior.

6. User Training

Educate users about the current system behavior and the steps they need to take to avoid selecting products marked for deletion until the issue is resolved.

Conclusion

Based on the information from SAP Note 3111463, the system should not display products marked for deletion in the search help/value help. If it does, it is likely a bug. Opening a support ticket with SAP is the best course of action to get an official fix. Meanwhile, consider implementing temporary workarounds to mitigate the issue.

entry.title: Re: Recording SAP GUI Dropdown in Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Mira, I ran your question through our Eerly AI SAP Consultant and got the below response.  I am interested to know if this answers your question.

SAP AI Consultant

Issue Overview

You are facing issues with recording a dropdown button in SAP GUI. When you attempt to record the interaction, the dropdown does not open, but it works normally once the recording is off. This is a common issue when using SAP GUI scripting or recording tools to capture interactions with dropdowns and other dynamic elements.

Steps to Address the Issue

  1. Use SAP GUI Scripting:

    • SAP GUI scripting can be used to automate interactions with SAP GUI elements, including dropdowns. This method allows you to script the exact steps needed to open the dropdown and select an option.

  2. Record in Steps:

    • Record the interaction in steps, capturing each action separately. This can sometimes help in capturing the dropdown interaction correctly.

  3. Manual Script Adjustment:

    • Manually adjust the recorded script to include the necessary commands to open the dropdown and select an option. This might involve adding or modifying the script commands.

Detailed Steps:

Step 1: Use SAP GUI Scripting

  • Enable SAP GUI Scripting:

    • Ensure that SAP GUI scripting is enabled on both the client and server sides. This can be done in the SAP Logon options and the SAP system settings.

  • Record Script:

    • Use the SAP GUI Scripting Recorder to record the interaction. This tool can be accessed from the SAP GUI options.

Step 2: Record in Steps

  • Record Dropdown Opening:

    • Start recording and perform the action to open the dropdown. Stop recording after this step.
    • Save the recorded script.

  • Record Option Selection:

    • Start a new recording and perform the action to select an option from the dropdown. Stop recording after this step.
    • Save the recorded script.

  • Combine Scripts:

    • Combine the two recorded scripts into a single script that performs both actions sequentially.

Step 3: Manual Script Adjustment

  • Edit Script:

    • Open the recorded script in a text editor or the SAP GUI Scripting IDE.
    • Locate the commands related to the dropdown interaction.

  • Add/Modify Commands:

    • Ensure that the script includes commands to open the dropdown and select an option. The commands might look something like this:
' Open the dropdown
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtFIELD_ID").setFocus
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtFIELD_ID").caretPosition = 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[17]").press

' Select an option from the dropdown
session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/lbl[OPTION_ID]").setFocus
session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/lbl[OPTION_ID]").press
  • Test Script:
    • Run the script to ensure that it correctly opens the dropdown and selects the desired option.

Example Script:

Here is an example script that demonstrates how to open a dropdown and select an option using SAP GUI scripting:

If Not IsObject(application) Then
   Set SapGuiAuto  = GetObject("SAPGUI")
   Set application = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
End If
If Not IsObject(connection) Then
   Set connection = application.Children(0)
End If
If Not IsObject(session) Then
   Set session    = connection.Children(0)
End If
If IsObject(WScript) Then
   WScript.ConnectObject session, "on"
   WScript.ConnectObject application, "on"
End If

' Open the dropdown
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtFIELD_ID").setFocus
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtFIELD_ID").caretPosition = 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[17]").press

' Select an option from the dropdown
session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/lbl[OPTION_ID]").setFocus
session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/lbl[OPTION_ID]").press

Conclusion

To record the interaction with a dropdown button in SAP GUI, you can use SAP GUI scripting to automate the process. Record the interaction in steps, manually adjust the script if necessary, and ensure that the commands to open the dropdown and select an option are correctly included. This approach should help you capture the desired interaction for your automation or recording purposes.

entry.title: Re: Why the Test Automation Tool (TAT) is a Better Choice...
entry.content:
Hello @vidishamohan23 ,
Let me try go give the difference and usecase for both.
So when you say that the customer wants to use the SAP Cloud ALM for manual testing. I believe for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud project Cloud ALM should definitely be considered for manual testing. However, please understand that Cloud ALM is not only used for Testing purpose. SAP Cloud ALM helps you to implement and operate intelligent cloud and hybrid business solutions, which means that your customer needs to understand the important functionality of the Cloud ALM which can be used for your project management activities.

Test management is a part of Cloud ALM where you can integrate the SAP Test Automation Tool with the Cloud ALM to have a better orchestration of your Manual and the automated testing.
Also, as you know that the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud provided a bi-yearly upgrade, where every 6 months you your systems get upgraded and it's a good practice to test your solution in your Test System during the 3 weeks window where you Test system is upgraded first. Now, manually testing your scenarios would eat up a lot of your time. Therefore, if you are using the Test Automation Tool and can used the automated testing, this would not only reduce your regression testing efforts but will also free up your time for other activities during the upgrade.

Sorry to the long answer, but it's necessary to understand the purpose of using both.
Offcourse, as per your last comment the Test Automation tool does not have the Comment section and the task assignment feature like the Cloud ALM, because the purpose of the test automation is testing your regression suite.

Hope this helps!

Regards,
Sonam

entry.title: DevSecOps for SAP S/4HANA Rise
entry.content:
In the modern digital world, businesses are turning more and more to cloud-based platforms to handle their essential operations. Among these, SAP S/4HANA RISE stands out as a powerful solution, enabling businesses to drive digital transformation, streamline operations, and innovate at scale. However, with the adoption of such a robust system, the importance of embedding security within every aspect of development and operations has never been more paramount. Enter DevSecOps, a modern approach that integrates security into the entire development pipeline, ensuring that the S/4HANA and RISE with SAP environments remain secure, resilient, and agile.

Strategic Importance of DevSecOps in Rise with SAP
DevSecOps is a paradigm shift in an enterprise's software development and operational capabilities, especially within the SAP S/4HANA and RISE framework. Integrating security into the DevOps pipeline ensures it becomes a core element of the development process rather than being considered later. This is particularly critical for SAP systems, which often serve as the operational backbone for many organizations.

SAP S/4HANA RISE, a cloud-based managed service, offers a unique environment that benefits from DevSecOps practices. The platform's inherent flexibility and scalability must be reinforced with strong security measures, continuous delivery, and automated testing to ensure compliance, security, and uninterrupted business operations.

Key Components of a DevSecOps Strategy
Implementing a DevSecOps strategy within an SAP S/4HANA RISE environment involves multiple layers of technology and best practices that ensure security, scalability, and performance. Outlined below are key elements of an effective DevSecOps framework:

 


1. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP):
SAP BTP plays a central role in facilitating DevSecOps within SAP environments. It serves as the foundation for building, deploying, and managing applications and extensions. This platform provides organizations with essential tools like API management, integration services, and extension services, which are critical to maintaining a flexible and secure application landscape. By leveraging BTP, organizations can ensure their SAP S/4HANA RISE environment is both scalable and compliant with DevSecOps principles. This alignment allows for custom applications to be developed without compromising security or system integrity.

2. Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD):
The CI/CD pipeline is a critical component of any DevSecOps strategy. It enables the seamless integration of code changes, automated testing, and deployment, ensuring that updates can be delivered quickly and securely. For SAP S/4HANA RISE, a well-designed CI/CD pipeline ensures that customizations, upgrades, and extensions are deployed efficiently, with minimal impact on business processes. Tools such as Jenkins, GitLab, and SAP's native CI/CD services play a pivotal role in automating and streamlining these processes, helping organizations to deliver high-quality solutions faster.

3. Clean Core Strategy:
Maintaining a clean core is another key aspect of an effective DevSecOps strategy for SAP S/4HANA. The clean core approach minimizes customizations within the core SAP system, pushing extensions and custom code into the BTP environment. This separation simplifies upgrades, reduces the total cost of ownership (TCO), and ensures that future patches and updates can be implemented without causing disruptions. By reducing complexity and adhering to a clean core strategy, organizations can keep their SAP environment secure, standardized, and easier to maintain.

4. Cloud ALM for DevSecOps:
SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management) is an all-encompassing solution designed to manage every phase of an application's lifecycle, from the early planning stages to implementation and continuous monitoring. When integrated with DevSecOps, Cloud ALM enables real-time monitoring and feedback loops, allowing organizations to continuously improve their SAP S/4HANA RISE environment. This proactive approach ensures that issues are detected early and resolved quickly, reducing downtime and improving system performance.

Implementation Roadmap and Expected Outcomes
Implementing DevSecOps for SAP S/4HANA RISE is not a one-off project-it is a continuous process that evolves as the needs of the business grow and change. The implementation begins with an assessment of the current capabilities, identifying gaps in security, agility, and performance. From there, a detailed roadmap is established, which includes the design and implementation of a robust CI/CD pipeline, integration of security and testing tools, and the adoption of a clean core strategy. By adopting this DevSecOps approach, organizations can expect the following outcomes:

Enhanced Security: Continuous security integration minimizes vulnerabilities and ensures compliance with industry standards.

Improved Agility: The CI/CD pipeline accelerates updates and new feature deployments, enabling rapid response to market shifts.

Lower TCO: The clean core strategy simplifies the SAP environment, reducing maintenance costs and easing future upgrades. Higher Quality: Automated testing and monitoring reduce the risk of defects and performance issues, ensuring system reliability.

Proactive Monitoring: Cloud ALM provides real-time visibility into system health and performance, allowing for proactive resolution of issues.

Conclusion
In conclusion, the DevSecOps strategy for SAP S/4HANA RISE offers a comprehensive framework for delivering secure, agile, and high-performing SAP environments. By integrating security, testing, and continuous delivery into every stage of the development lifecycle, organizations can not only protect their SAP systems but also drive business innovation and growth. To fully leverage their SAP S/4HANA RISE investment, companies must embrace a DevSecOps approach as a key strategy for ensuring long-term success and staying competitive in the market.

entry.title: SAC Planning BPC live model - Publish data from the script issue
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

I'm working in an SAC Planning environment connected to a live BPC embedded model from BW. I use buttons with scripts to run several planning sequences together with data saves (publish data) in the same step, and it works fine when I'm working with the model and a table in input mode. However, I've noticed that it doesn't recognize that the version's data has been modified for a story where I run a planning sequence and a data save, where the table is not input-ready. It seems like there's a delay between the end of the sequence and the data published. As a workaround, I've added a refresh step between the sequence execution and the publish step, and it works that way. Has anyone else experienced this? Any other solutions?

This is the code:

BPC_PlanningSequence.execute();

//This refresh is introduced with the purpose of ensuring that it recognizes 
Table_1.getDataSource().refreshData();

var bpcVersion = Table_1.getPlanning().getPublicVersion("All Versions");
if (bpcVersion) {
if(bpcVersion.isDirty()) {
bpcVersion.publish();
};
};

Best Regards,

Rubén

entry.title: SAP CAP - How to create a custom endpoint?
entry.content:
I would like to improve my CAP service by adding custom endpoints with business logic.

I don't know how to do it correctly, I'm calling the created endpoint but it's not returning anything to me. I am sharing my implementation with you to see if you can help me. I just want to try something basic, like being able to get an object/entity using an ID sent by parameter.

entities_srv.cds:

using { app.entities } from '../db/entities';

service BTP6SService
{
    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity EMPRESA as
        projection on entities.EMPRESA;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity PERSONA as
        projection on entities.PERSONA;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity AREANIVEL as
        projection on entities.AREANIVEL;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity AREA as
        projection on entities.AREA;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity SECTOR as
        projection on entities.SECTOR;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity EQUIPO as
        projection on entities.EQUIPO;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity TIPORESPUESTA as
        projection on entities.TIPORESPUESTA;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity CUESTIONARIO as
        projection on entities.CUESTIONARIO;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity PREGUNTA as
        projection on entities.PREGUNTA;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity VISITA as
        projection on entities.VISITA;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity VISITACUESTIONARIO as
        projection on entities.VISITACUESTIONARIO;

    @requires: 'Admin'
    entity VISITACUESTIONARIOPREGUNTA as
        projection on entities.VISITACUESTIONARIOPREGUNTA;

    @requires: 'Visitante'
    entity NOTICIA as
        projection on entities.NOTICIA;

    @requires: 'Visitante'
    entity NOTICIAMEDIA as
        projection on entities.NOTICIAMEDIA;

    @requires: 'Visitante'
    entity NOTICIAPERSONALIKE as
        projection on entities.NOTICIAPERSONALIKE;

    action getEmpresaById(id: String) returns EMPRESA;
}

annotate BTP6SService with @requires :
[
    'authenticated-user'
];

server.js:

"use strict";

const cds = require("@sap/cds");

const cors = require("cors");

const { SELECT } = cds.ql;

cds.on("bootstrap", app => app.use(cors()));

cds.on("ready", () => {
    const { BTP6SService } = cds.services;

    BTP6SService.on("getEmpresaById", async (req) => {
        const id = req.data.id;

        const empresas = await cds.run(SELECT.from("entities.EMPRESA").where({ ID: id }));

        if (empresas.length === 0) {
            req.error(404, `Empresa con ID ${id} no encontrada.`);
        }

        return empresas[0];
    });
});

module.exports = cds.server;

This is how I am calling the endpoint in my React Native app, definition and usage:

const getEmpresaById = async (id, token) => {
    try {
      const response = await axios.post(
        "https://769f798etrial-emorales-ebwzszn2-dev-btp6s-srv.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/odata/v4/btp6-s/getEmpresaById",
        { id: id },
        {
          headers: {
            Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "x-requested-with": "XMLHttpRequest",
          },
        }
      );

      // Procesa la respuesta
      console.log(response.data);
      return response.data;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Error al obtener la empresa:", error);
      throw error;
    }
  };
getEmpresaById("8d7a5b2e-4c9f-3e6a-9d2b-5f1e7a3c6b9d", token)
        .then((empresa) => {
          console.log("Empresa obtenida:", empresa);
        })
        .catch((error) => {
          console.error("Error:", error);
        });

Empty result:

entry.title: Re: create Email template in C4C?
entry.content:
Hi Nikos,

You can create Email templates in SAP C4C
1. at Sales -> Templates -> All Workflow Notification Templates

Click on the "+" icon, Create the template based file and add the html template file.

 

2. Once created the email template you can use the email template in the workflows

To maintain the template - you can create Email type workflow and add the templates.

 

Thanks

Niveditha

Hi, 
I am not sure why only those two browsers are listed, but I think you will be fine with Edge as well (in the background it runs chromium anyways...)

The latest feature scope description for SAP Build Apps has Edge and Safari listed as well:
https://help.sap.com/doc/2acecfb777684ce5a0a8a75fd78caffb/Latest/en-US/2a23b2bcd9084d83b9a8e76b2d1b4372.pdf

The documentation for SAP Build Process Automation lists Edge together with Crome even as reference platform (based on my personal experience MacUsers do face some limitations with some parts of this solution).
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/system-requirements-and-technical-prerequisites

SAP Build Workzone references SAPUI5 supported browsers... Edge = no problem
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/supported-browsers

Best regards, Franz

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP

First heard of SAP whilst at university as an undergrad in 1985-6, with some job openings - but it seemed too much of an business solution, than the technology I'd studied.

It wasn't until 1999 that SAP cropped up again, with an opportunity to get involved, when I was at a UK bank deploying R/3 4.6B - Being a bank they wanted to keep the SAP security part in-house, and the rest, so they say, is history (25 years deploying SAP securely).

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Hybrid Connector - Classic vs New Product co...
entry.content:
Hello Paulo

i am not allowed to share details of 3E adapter. But the concept used on Product Compliance (regarding SVT) and in SVT EHS Classic does not really fit (i skip to list the details). To use the 3E Adapter: get in touch with 3E direct please. There are some prerequisites. Only if you can satisfy this.. it makes sense to use 3E adapter.

UNtil that point in time... we have to use SVT EHS Classic; the process in Product Compliance is partially different to EHS classic. So it is not easy to map both solutions. The 3E Adapter is an option.

As we can expect (assumption) November 2025 next "on prem" version... sometimes we must wait (or we must use "On Cloid" variant.

C.B.

entry.title: CPI: API has restriction to pull only 100 records in each call

There is a source system and source system is exposing an API and CPI needs to get the data from source system and send it to target system(some third party). But they are some restrictions defined in the source system API level to pick the data.
1st restriction is it can give only 100 records in a call not more than that.
2nd restriction is sender API itself can be called only once per minute.
but i have close to 10k records from source application which needs to transfered to target application.

Please explain how to achieve this in CPI level? it would great helpful if you answer.

entry.title: Different types of SAP BTP environments and how to choose the right one
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is an integrated offering comprised of four technology portfolios: database & data management, application development & integration, analytics, and intelligent technologies. The platform allows users to turn data into business value, compose end-to-end business processes, and quickly build and extend SAP applications.

SAP BTP comes in two variants:Neo, which is being gradually phased out, and Multi-Cloud, which has been the default for new customers since 2020. SAP BTP lets you choose from different infrastructures and runtimes. Its services and solutions are available on multiple cloud infrastructure providers; it supports different runtimes, such as Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Kyma, as well as multiple regions and a broad choice of programming languages.

In thisblog, I will explain the difference between each environment and how to choose the right one.

SAP BTP Environments

The environment in SAP BTP is a platform-as-a-service offering that offers runtime, services, and tools for developing and executing a business application. Environments are chosen at the subaccount level.

SAP BTP comes in two variants, offering different environments:

  1. Multi-Cloud
    • Cloud Foundry Environment:
      • The Cloud Foundry environment contains the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime service, based on the open-source application platform managed by the Cloud Foundry Foundation.
      • It enables developers to develop new business applications and business services, supporting multiple runtimes, programming languages (Node.js, Java, Python, etc.), libraries, and services.
      • Scales easily and supports DevOps pipelines.
      • It offers a broad range of services like machine learning, IoT, and SAP AI.
    • ABAP Environment:
      • The ABAP environment allows developers to create extensions for ABAP-based products, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and develop new cloud applications.
      • It supports the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model, which includes SAP Fiori and Core Data Services (CDS).
      • Ideal for extending on-premise SAP systems.
    • Kyma Environment:
      • The Kyma environment is a fully managed Kubernetes runtime based on the open-source project "Kyma."
      •  It allows developers to extend SAP solutions with serverless functions and combine them with containerised microservices.
      • Supports serverless capabilities and event triggers.
      • Supports autoscaling and self-healing.
      • Ideal for event-driven architectures.
  2. Neo
    • Neo Environment:
      • The Neo environment is provisioned from SAP data centers.
      • It enables developers to develop HTML5, Java, and SAP HANA extended application services (SAP HANA XS) applications.
      • SAP has announced that the Neo environment will be phased out on December 31, 2028, subject to terms of customer or partner contracts.

Comparison: SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime and SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime

        

Category

SAP BTP, Kyma runtime

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime

Based on open source

Based on Kyma open source

Based on Cloud Foundry open source

Developer profile

Developers skilled in Java, Node.JS, Python, and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), including SAP UI5 and Fiori Elements.

Developers skilled in Java, Node.JS, Python, and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), including SAP UI5 and Fiori Elements.

Micro Services

Yes

Yes

CAP Applications

Yes

Yes

SAP Cloud SDK support

Yes

Yes

Approuter support

Standalone approuter

Both Standalone and Managed Approuter

Supported buildpacks

Cloud-native buildpacks

SAP-managed and Cloud Foundry community buildpacks for several development languages.

Service mesh support

Yes

No

Elasticity

Yes

Yes

Autoscaling support

Yes

Yes, with the Application Autoscaler service.

In-system build & deploy using buildpacks

No

Yes

Platform-as-a-service: MTA support

No

Yes

Transport services

No

Yes

Minimum infrastructure size

3 Kubernetes Nodes, each 4 CPU and 16 GB memory. Roughly 40% is reserved to run the infrastructure.

1 GB Memory with ¼ CPU core.

How to choose the right environment – Cloud Foundry vs ABAP vs Kyma

It is essential to choose the right environment for your BTP account. Here are a few points to consider:

  • Evaluate your application requirements:
    • If your application needs multi-cloud support and scalability, then Cloud Foundry is likely the best choice because of its multi-cloud and microservices capabilities.
    • If you are extending an existing ABAP-based SAP system, then ABAP Environment is the most suitable.
    • If you are building event-driven, serverless, or containerised applications, Kyma Environment would be the best choice as it supports Kubernetes and microservice architectures.
  • Programming language and technology stack preferences:
    • Cloud Foundry supports various languages like Java, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, and PHP.
    • Kyma is Kubernetes-based and supports containers, microservices, and serverless functions.
    • ABAP Environment uses ABAP and is ideal for customers with an existing SAP ABAP codebase.
  • Assess your integration needs: Choose the environment based on the requirement to integrate with other SAP systems or third-party services. 
    • Cloud Foundry integrates easily with various SAP and non-SAP services.
    • ABAP Environment integrates smoothly with existing SAP systems, especially S/4HANA.
    • Kyma supports both event-based integration and Kubernetes orchestration, which is ideal for extending cloud applications.
  • Consider scalability and cloud provider options:
    • Cloud Foundry allows deployment on multiple cloud providers like AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.
    • Kyma supports Kubernetes, which is highly scalable for containerised workloads.
    • ABAP Environment is SAP-managed and designed for extending SAP environments.
  • Evaluate cost and pricing: Different environments have varying cost implications depending on:
    • Services you require.
    • Your cloud provider of choice.
    • The scale of your application.

Conclusion: Which Environment to Choose?

  • Cloud Foundry: If you need multi-cloud support, microservices, and broad programming language support.
  • Kyma: If you are building event-driven, Kubernetes-based applications or using serverless architecture.
  • ABAP Environment: If you are extending SAP’s core applications (like S/4HANA) and working with an ABAP-heavy stack.

Best Regards,

Sudhir.

entry.title: Custom Analytical Query Input Filter
entry.content:
Hello SAP, 

We are trying to create a report. We have created a Custom CDS View and on top of it we have created a Custom Analytical Query.

Is it possible to include selection screen input field with two values to choose from 1. MISC and 2. NEC?

We want selection screen with two values to choose from could you please suggest.

Thanks and Regards. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom Analytical Query Input Filter
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

If you want the filter to appear in the Custom Analytical Query header when previewing it, you need to choose the filter type "User Input Values". Upon query execution, this option enables end users to choose a value that filters the result set. 

However, the selection as a Value Help is not possible. The user will need to input manually the value that he wants to filter in the filter field. 

 

For more information, you can check SAP Help Potal | Defining Filters

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK OData binding navigation pages
entry.content: In the tutorial you set the Page level Design Time Target. This is to let the editor know what the expected default binding will be for the Object Browser to help filter down the choices.  Unless I missed it, I don't believe the tutorial has you specify a target for the Object Header or the Key Value Section on the Detail page.  For both of those you are just using the default binding object passed into the page.

entry.title: Need responses for some use cases of SAP Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on some test cases related to the CIM solution for one of our clients, and I’ve noticed that the solution does not handle certain scenarios correctly, revealing some limitations.

Case of an invoice with an additional item:

I observed that even when configuring the account management rule (supplier, GL account, and cost center for the determination of accounts for items without reference), the result is as follows: only the item with a purchase order is automatically added. The second item must be manually added, either to a general account or by searching for an existing purchase order in the system. There is no automatic account determination for the second item.

Case of an invoice with a supplier Delivery Note number:

The system does not recognize the delivery note number to perform automatic matching or accounting, unlike when a purchase order number is provided as a reference.

Could you please help me understand the strengths and weaknesses of the CIM solution, and how we should handle cases like utility or phone invoices with multiple lines (detailed consumption and a total amount at the end)? Would creating a specific template for these types of invoices be a good approach? How can we implement it? Also, if this template is loaded into CIM, will other vendor invoices still be processed by the same OCR system?

My client has high expectations regarding CIM, and I am very frustrated that CIM cannot meet these expectations.
BR 

Yassir

entry.title: Don’t Miss Database Sessions at SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is right around the corner and includes a dynamic lineup of keynote presentations and interactive jumpstart sessions across SAP’s Data and Analytics portfolio, including SAP HANA Cloud. For database enthusiasts who enjoy solving business challenges and empowering users with top-tier tools, be sure to register for the sessions highlighted later in this blog.


A Short Note About the Next Generation of Applications

Chances are, anyone reading this article is likely familiar with using, maintaining, or dealing with legacy applications. As these systems age, their support costs grow while business needs evolve beyond the application’s original scope. These issues often leave IT decision-makers in a difficult position, constantly weighing the benefits of maintaining the status quo against the need for innovation. The unfortunate and simple truth regarding legacy applications is that they are no longer adequate for today’s business needs. To bridge this gap, IT teams must transition to intelligent data applications and utilize the full power of SAP's in-memory database. Features such as data-tiering, multi-model engines, and AI libraries enable today's custom solutions to handle a new generation of business challenges.

At SAP TechEd 2024, four feature presentations showcase intelligent data applications using SAP HANA Cloud as the database. Learn more about these virtual sessions below and make sure to register today!


DA101 | SAP HANA Cloud: Recent innovations, road map, and strategy 
Tuesday, Oct 8, 11:30 AM - 11:55 AM CET / 5:30 AM - 5:55 AM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

As businesses innovate to meet evolving demands and drive efficiency, staying ahead of data infrastructure advancements is crucial. Join Thomas Hammer for an in-depth look at the latest innovations in SAP HANA Cloud, including new vector capabilities that integrate generative AI into enterprise solutions. This session also covers the latest road map and strategy, offering insights into creating a scalable, future-proof database platform with SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.


DA102 | AI-enabled clean core with SAP HANA Cloud 
Tuesday, Oct 8, 3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CET / 9:30 AM - 9:55 AM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

Maintaining stable and consistent core ERP applications while driving innovation is a key challenge for many businesses today. In this session, Volker Saggau explains SAP’s clean core strategy and demonstrates how it helps global companies keep their ERP systems up-to-date and unmodified without comprising system integrity. With the support of SAP HANA Cloud’s multi-model and multi-tenancy capabilities, SAP customer Henkel shares how they balance system hygiene with innovation by leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform to power their $12 billion revenue environment.


DA103 | Generative AI-driven business transformation enabled by SAP HANA Cloud
Wednesday, Oct 9, 6:30 PM - 6:55 PM CET / 12:30 PM - 12:55 PM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

As AI evolves, organizations need robust solutions for business transformation. Join Christoph Morgen and Markus Fath for a session that explores how SAP HANA Cloud supports the development of generative AI applications on the SAP Business Technology Platform. Key topics include multi-model capabilities like the vector engine, insights into SAP HANA’s AI function libraries, and a practical use case involving SAP Joule.


DA181 | Build AI-enabled intelligent data applications to help keep the core clean 
Wednesday, Oct 9, 7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CET / 1:30 PM - 1:55 PM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

In the pursuit of innovation, organizations should ensure their core ERP systems remain unmodified and consistent. This session discusses the concept of clean core and its role in supporting custom applications alongside their ERP systems. Key topics include how SAP HANA Cloud powers the development of AI-enabled intelligent data applications using its vector engine as well as the upcoming knowledge graph engine. The session also highlights how code generation within SAP Build speeds up the development process.


Build and Deploy Intelligent Data Applications Today

Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud. Supplement these SAP TechEd sessions with first-hand experience using the SAP HANA Cloud free basic trial. Click here to get started.

entry.title: Involved Party Sales manager in the Activity tasks of the Sales orders are incorrect
entry.content:
Hi,

 

When the sales order is set to "Shipped" status by a user an activity task will be created, but the user who has set the status as Shipped will be fetched as "Sales manager" in the Involved parties of the task, which is incorrect.

The involved parties for the tasks of Sales orders are not determining from the Sales order Involved parties.

Action taken from our end:
Under Business configuration--->Implementation project--->Open Activity list---> Activities---->Maintain Involved parties for the task----> Sales manager---->Maintain determination,
We could see the current user is checked, so because of which the Sales manager is fetching as a user who ever changes the status.
We have tried maintaining the other determination values for Sales manager role, but none of them is fetching the Involved party maintained in the Sales order.


The Involved parties of role Sales manager of the Activity tasks of Sales order should be the same as the related Sales Order. This is happening in all the tenants.

Could you please help us to fix this, as this is affecting all the business users?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Involved Party Sales manager in the Activity tasks of...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

It is currently not possible to inherit the Sales Manager party role in Tasks from Sales Order, only from Account. This is the expected system behavior.

 

Thanks,

SAP Support

entry.title: Cool fade in fade out for adding new entries in table (ui5)
entry.content:
TL;DR

I have a table on the left hand side where I can select an entry. This entry is being handled by a function that pushes the record to the other table (nothing fancy here!). Under the hood we post the entry to a odata service to store it as a favorite in this case. After storing, retrieve all the information back to make sure everything is updated. After the updated version, we select the added record and mark it specially (frontend only). 

 
This onSelectNetwork event selects the current record and posts it to a Favorites odata service to add the new entry (if it is not there already!):
 

First it checks if the selected item is already in the favorites list. If not, let's add it. After saving, we get all the favorites again from backend. Now let's find the added record and mark it also as a new record (

favorite.csstransition = "transition")

 All other records will get the csstransition property as well, but it will be set to empty. This property will be used to do some magic!

 
But before that we need to make sure that our right table is listening to new magic!
The customData makes sure we add something nice there. There is a binding to the new property csstransition (which is for old records empty and for newly created ones set to "transition". You can name it as you want, the two things you need to use are the key and the value names.

 

 

CSS

Add to your custom css file the following lines:

 

That's it!

Your newly added record will be marked as transition, the css will do the trick for 3 seconds. You can play with it ofcourse. I recently noticed and see it on internet as well while ordering that new lines are visually added to your basket. Love to think how we can integrate this more in UI5 and make it part of the framework.

entry.title: Re: FCP para itens com ST e Clientes Contribuintes
entry.content:
Boa tarde, 

Eu estou com um cenário em que o cliente vende energia, ele é consumidor final, mas a empresa trata tudo como industrialização. Preciso calcular o ICMS ST e FCP ST, ja ativei todas as condições precisas, a operação não possui ICMS proprio. 

Quando coloco o codigo de imposto de consumo na operação, calcula corretamente o FCP ST, porem a base ICMS ST não é a que queremos. 

Quando coloco o codigo de imposto para industrialização, ele não realiza o calculo do FCP ST somente do ICMS ST. 

Alguem ja passou por isso? tentei encontrar notas que me ajudasse mas não acho.

entry.title: Strange character issue in SAP master data table
entry.content:
Introduction: I recently encountered an odd problem when reading data from the material master database table. It had a few strange characters. Though I couldn't see anything in the GUI with my bare eyes except some spaces (Image 1), something was buried behind the spaces. However, it was transmitting all wacky characters (Image 2) when we started to send the description to the third-party system. As a result, the third-party system was broken and was unable to evaluate the data.

Image 1:

Image 2: 

Solution: To eliminate all of these odd characters from the description, we can utilize SAP's Function Module 'SCP_REPLACE_STRANGE_CHARS'. It works on the codepage principle. To eliminate all of these characters, we simply set replacement as 32, the ASCII character of space. Additionally, for the the correct result, we must pass inter_cp as '1101' and inter_base_cp as '4110'. 

 

 

DATA: lv_maktx TYPE maktx,
      lv_outtext TYPE maktx.
CALL FUNCTION 'SCP_REPLACE_STRANGE_CHARS'
        EXPORTING
          intext            = lv_maktx
          inter_cp          = '1101'
          inter_base_cp     = '4110'
          replacement       = 32
        IMPORTING
          outtext           = lv_outtext
        EXCEPTIONS
          invalid_codepage  = 1
          codepage_mismatch = 2
          internal_error    = 3
          cannot_convert    = 4
          fields_not_type_c = 5
          OTHERS            = 6.
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
      ENDIF.

 

 

And as you can see below, the final output no longer contains any odd alias characters that appear goofy.

I sincerely hope you found this content interesting to read. At any moment, I would welcome your opinions and recommendations. And once again, many thanks for your valuable time.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: Is it ok to use req._query[$filter] in a custom handler?
entry.content:
Hi @PedroLazaro!
Have you seen this example on GitHub?
In this Risk Management application, the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER external service is used. And if you look, there in the schema Suppliers is a projection to this external:

using { API_BUSINESS_PARTNER as bupa } from '../srv/external/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER';

entity Suppliers as projection on bupa.A_BusinessPartner {


And if you look at the service implementation, you can see that for READ operations on this Suppliers entity, in some cases, it runs the query from the request on the external API_BUSINESS_PARTNER:

this.on('READ', 'Suppliers', async req => {
return bupa.run(req.query);
});



This might be appropriate for you, or how do you see you can use this?

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2024 #SITCWB
entry.content:
Temos o prazer em anunciar que a Nova Consulting irá patrocinar o evento SAP Inside Track, que será realizado a sua segunda edição em Curitiba.

O SAP Inside Track é o maior evento independente de tecnologia SAP do Brasil. É uma iniciativa dos membros da Comunidade SAP e já tem presença em mais de 50 cidades em todo o mundo. Apenas no Brasil, o evento já contou com 20 edições em 10 cidades diferentes: São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Novo Hamburgo, Campinas, Recife, Salvador, Belo Horizonte e Ribeirão Preto. Confira as palestras destes eventos através do canal do youtube SIT Brasil.

O maior objetivo deste evento é compartilhar conhecimento, aprender e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP, por meio de palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócio. Esta é a maior oportunidade conhecer grandes profissionais SAP pessoalmente, para estreitar o seu networking, ficando por dentro de todas as inovações que envolvem o SAP, S/4 HANA e outras soluções.

Não fique de fora deste evento!

Se prepare para aprender, compartilhar, conhecer outros profissionais e as pessoas do www.sap.com/community que você ainda não conhece pessoalmente.

Local e Data

  • Onde:  FAE Business School
    • Av. Visc. de Guarapuava, 3263 - Centro, Curitiba - PR, 80010-100
  • Quando: Sábado, 23 Novembro, 2024
  • Horário: 09:00 – 17:30.
  • Quem: Profissionais da área SAP.
  • Custo:  R$ 30,00
  • Email de Contato: sapinsidetrackbrazil@gmail.com
  • Oferecemos: Coffee Break manhã e tarde e sorteio de brindes

Participe e nos ajude a fazer a diferença

O valor arrecadado com as inscrições será 100% doado.

 

FAÇA SUA INSCRIÇÃO CLICANDO AQUI

 

Participante:

A taxa de inscrição do evento é de R$ 30, e inclui:

  • Entrada no evento;
  • Acesso as apresentações das sessões apresentadas;

 

Palestrante:

Aproveite esta oportunidade para compartilhar o seu conhecimento e nos ajuda a tornar ainda melhor este evento.

  • Deverá ser enviado uma proposta com o tema e uma sinopse.
  • O tempo de palestra é de no máximo 40 minutos.
  • O último dia para envio de propostas é sexta-feira, 25 de Outubro.
  • Aguarde o contato da nossa equipe até o dia 01 de Novembro.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: Wrong input for Date Type
entry.content:
Hi @abranjan,

I have now looked at the documentation of the DATE datatype, and I see that blank is not a valid value, because it can only be a bound format for the date (YYYY-MM-DD), and if null is allowed, then the null value can still be valid, and no other value can be entered. This is what the error message describes.

Can you explain why it would be relevant to have a blank value? Would this be necessary during some data processing?

the standard approach for it is exactly the status management. Anything beyond, or done other way around will require development. There is a customer exit, which you can leverage : IWO10009.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Elastic Scaling of Application Servers
entry.content: An ABAP Environment system is always assigned to exactly one subaccount and one related CF space. This subaccount determines the region where the system is provisioned and which Identity Provider (IDP) is used for authentication. But beside this you can share one ABAP Environment system with different BTP services from other accounts like SAP Business Application Studio.

entry.title: Exploring Accrual Engine By Examples Part 2 - Purchase Order Accruals
entry.content:
Welcome to Part 2 of our series on the Accrual Engine. In this installment, we'll delve into Purchase Order (PO) Accruals, exploring how to manage and automate the accrual process using practical examples. Understanding PO accruals is crucial for accurate financial reporting and ensuring that expenses are recognized in the appropriate accounting periods.

1. Introduction

Purchase Order Accruals refer to the accounting practice of recognizing expenses related to purchase orders before the actual invoice is received. This ensures that expenses are recorded in the correct accounting period, adhering to the matching principle in accrual accounting.

PO accruals are crucial for ensuring accurate financial reporting by recognizing expenses in the correct accounting periods, aligning with the matching principle. This practice enhances compliance with standards like GAAP or IFRS, improves cash flow visibility by reflecting liabilities accurately, and provides a reliable audit trail for transparency. Ultimately, PO accruals support better financial planning and regulatory adherence.

2. Example

A Company purchased 10 units of material Trading Good 0011 at a net price of 100 EUR per unit, totaling 1,000 EUR. The goods will be delivered later, and since the goods haven't arrived and no invoice has been received, the Accrual Engine triggers the process to recognize the expense in the current period.

a. Create a Purchase Order (PO)

The Order Type of the PO should be maintained for accrual management in transaction code ACEIMG.

Two points:

  • The 'Account Assignement Category' should be 'K' (Cost Center).
  • In this example, the 'Goods Receipt Indicator' is selected under the 'Delivery' tab. We will revist this point later.

b. Check the related Accural Object

After the PO is created, an Accrual Object also will be created.

Open transaction code POACTREE03.

Input the 'Company Code' used in the PO and the PO number.

Then we can see the Accrual Object.

We can see there are three Accrual Item Types.

  • SACCRL (Standard Accrual):
    This is used for standard accrual postings, where expenses or revenues need to be recognized before the actual transaction (e.g., receipt of goods or services, invoice receipt). It ensures that the expense is recorded in the appropriate period, aligning with the accrual accounting principles.

  • SCSTACT (Cost Actual):
    This item type is used for actual cost postings related to an accrual. It reflects the real cost incurred in a transaction, helping to match expenses with revenues and providing a clear view of actual expenses in financial reporting.

  • SCSTPLN (Cost Planned):
    This type is used for planned cost postings. It is utilized for accrual scenarios where planned costs are posted to ensure proper financial forecasting and budgeting. It helps in comparing actual costs against planned figures during accrual postings.

The Planned cost (SCSTPLN) amount is 1000 Euro (purchased 10 materials and each material's price is 100 Euro).

The Actual cost (SCSTACT) amount is 0 Euro.

Why? This is becasue we selected 'Goods Receipt Indicator' in the PO. It means the Accrual Engine identify the amount will not be actually paid out until the related Good Receipt is created.

c. Propose Periodic Amounts

Different from Manual Accrual, PO Accrual needs run the 'Propose Periodic Amounts' first before post the accrual amount.

Open transaction code ACEPROPOSALRUN.

Input the PO number as the 'Accrual Object'.

From the result list, we can see the planned amount is 1000 Euro and actual amount is 0 Euro.

d. Post Accrual Amount

After the propose run, now we can run the arrual post.

Open transaction code ACEPOSTINGRUN.

Also input the PO number as the Accrual Object.

From the result list, we can see a FI document is posted.

Double like the document number, a popup screen will display.

Doble click the Accounting document number.

From the document header, we can see the document is created by Accrual Engine.

Now, let's take a look on the G/L accounts used in the document.

12561000 -> Accrual Offset account maintained in transaction ACEIMG.

51600000 -> Account maintained in the PO

e. Create Good Receipt for the PO

Open transaction code MIGO.

Input the PO number, click the 'Item OK' button and then save the document.

f. Rerun the Post Accrual Amounts

Open transaction code ACEPOSTINGRUN and run the post again.

From the result, we can see now the Accrual Amount changes from 0 to 1000 Euro (as the related Good Receipt created).

A new FI document is created with the post.

Double click the FI document number.

It looks very similar to the last document right?

We can see the main different is the Posting Key.

First time post:

Second time post:

This means the second document is a reversal document.

g. Check the Accrual Object 

Let's recheck the Accrual Object created for the PO.

Open transaction code POACTREE03.

Now, we can see the actual cost amount changes to 1000 Euro.

3. Conclusion

In this part of the series, we explored how the Accrual Engine automates Purchase Order (PO) accruals to ensure accurate expense recognition. Through a practical example, we demonstrated how the system triggers accruals, tracks planned and actual costs, and updates financial statements even before goods are received. Key steps like creating a PO, proposing periodic amounts, posting accruals, and adjusting entries after goods receipt show how the Accrual Engine streamlines the accrual process and improves financial accuracy.

Follow me on LinkedIn to stay updated on more financial and technical posts.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/zhengzhang-jason-lu-42574b41/
